notes: Bermuda should score English as fully compatible because English is the official and practical public language for travel.

evidence.title: Britannica: Bermuda
evidence.note: Britannica lists English as Bermuda's official language.

evidence.title: Government of Bermuda immigration English proficiency requirements
evidence.note: Government policy uses English as the required public-language standard for immigration and work-permit purposes.

notes: Caribbean Netherlands is an app-level aggregate of Bonaire, Sint Eustatius, and Saba. Dutch is the official administrative language, English dominates on Saba and St Eustatius, and Papiamentu is central on Bonaire.

evidence.title: CBS: Main languages of the Caribbean Netherlands
evidence.note: CBS shows English as the main language on Saba and St Eustatius, while Papiamentu is the main language on Bonaire.

evidence.title: Government.nl: Papiamento on Bonaire officially recognised
evidence.note: The Dutch government formally recognises Papiamento on Bonaire.

evidence.title: InfoBonaire language guide
evidence.note: Tourism guidance for Bonaire says Dutch is official, Papiamentu is the native majority language, and English is also widely spoken.

notes: Curaçao is fully multilingual for travelers: Papiamentu, Dutch, and English are official, and Spanish is also commonly usable.

evidence.title: Curaçao official tourism: native and official languages
evidence.note: Official tourism content says Dutch, Papiamentu, and English are official languages in Curaçao.

evidence.title: Curaçao official tourism: English is widely spoken
evidence.note: Official tourism content says most Curaçaoans speak English, Dutch, Spanish, and Papiamentu.

notes: Sint Maarten should not score as Dutch-only. English is official and dominant in daily life, with French and Spanish often usable in tourism-heavy settings.

evidence.title: Visit St. Maarten traveler information
evidence.note: Official tourism guidance says Dutch is used in government on the Dutch side, English is widely spoken everywhere, and Spanish, Creole, and Papiamento are also widely spoken.

evidence.title: Sint Maarten policy document language profile
evidence.note: Government planning material lists English and Dutch as official and shows English as the dominant public language.

notes: The Cayman Islands should score English as fully compatible because English is the official public language for travelers.

evidence.title: Cayman Islands official tourism FAQs
evidence.note: Official tourism FAQs state that English is the official language of the Cayman Islands.

notes: Gibraltar should score English as fully compatible while also recognizing that Spanish is widely usable in everyday local conversation.

evidence.title: Visit Gibraltar general information
evidence.note: Official tourism guidance says English is the official language and Spanish is widely spoken.

evidence.title: Britannica: Gibraltar
evidence.note: Britannica says most Gibraltarians are bilingual in English and Spanish.
